title: System and method for creating and using a health risk profile of a patient
abstract: A method of creating a local risk database for improving the speed of determining a health risk profile associated with a patient may include retrieving patient medical information about a first patient. The method may also include performing a first data conversion procedure including: sending the patient medical information to a translation resource if there is no match between the patient medical information and the records in the database; receiving translated patient medical information from the translation resource; sending the translated patient medical information to a coding resource; and receiving, from the coding resource, a predetermined code associated with the translated medical information. Also, the method may include using the predetermined code to determine a patient health risk profile and adding to the database a record of an association between the predetermined code and the patient medical information.
claims: Claims WHAT IS C LA! IVIED IS: 1. A method of creating a local risk database for improving the speed of determining a health risk profile associated with a patient comprising the steps of: retrieving patient medical information about a first patient, wherein the patient medical information is an uncoded natural language expression in a first language; performing a first data conversion procedure including: sending the patient medical information to a translation resource if there is no match between the patient medical information and the records in the database; receiving translated patient medical information from the translation resource, wherein the translated patient medical information is in a second language; sending the translated patient medical information to a coding resource; and receiving, from the coding resource, a predetermined code associated with the translated medical information; using the predetermined code to determine a health risk profile for the patient; and adding to the database a record of an association between the predetermined code and the patient medical information. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the predetermined code is a diagnosis code. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the predetermined code is a procedure code. 4. The method of claim 1 , further including comparing the patient medical information with records in the database. 5. The method of claim 4, further including making a determination of a level of confidence that the patient medical information matches the predetermined code. 6. The method of claim 5, further including repeating the steps of claim 1 for a second patient wherein the patient medical information for the second patient is similar to the patient medical information of the first patient; updating the database to include another record of an association between the predetermined code and the patient medical information; and defining an increased level of confidence in matches between the predetermined code and the patent medical information based on the number of records reflecting an association between the predetermined code and the patient medical information. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein determining if the patient medical information matches one of the records in the first database is performed by determining whether the level of confidence exceeds a first predetermined threshold. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the first data conversion procedure is performed on the patient medical information of the second patient if the level of confidence does not exceed the first predetermined threshold. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein a second data conversion procedure is performed on the patient medical information of the second patient if the levei of confidence exceeds the first predetermined threshold and does not exceed a second predetermined threshold, wherein the second data conversion procedure is a faster process than the first data conversion procedure; and performing a third data conversion procedure on the patient medical information of the second patient if the level of confidence exceeds the second predetermined threshold, wherein the third data conversion procedure is a faster process than the second data conversion procedure. 10. The method of claim 1 , further including a determining whether the patient medical information has no counterpart in a code system from which the predetermined code is selected. 11. The method of claim 10, further including assigning a best-fit predetermined code from the code system to the patient medical information for which no counterpart is provided in the code system. 12. The method of claim 11 , further including updating the database to include a record of an association between the best-fit predetermined code and the patient medical information for which no counterpart is provided in the code system. 13. The method of claim 12, further including assigning a custom code to the patient medical information for which no counterpart is provided in the code system. 14. The method of claim 13, further including updating the database to include a record of an association between the custom code and the patient medical information for which no counterpart is provided in the code system. 15. A method of patient communication based on a heaith risk profile, comprising the steps of: retrieving patient medical information about the patient, determining a heaith risk profile for the patient based on the retrieved patient medical information; producing an automated follow-up with a patient based on the heaith risk profile. 16. The method of claim 15, further including assigning a predetermined code associated with the preselected medical information to the patient medical information and determining the health risk profile based at least in part on the predetermined code. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the patient medical information is an uncoded natural language expression in a first language, the method further including: sending the patient medical information to a translation resource; receiving translated patient medical information from the translation resource, wherein the translated patient medical information is in a second language; sending the translated patient medical information to a coding resource; and receiving, from the coding resource, the predetermined code associated with the patient medical information. 18. A method of patient communication based on a heaith risk profile, comprising the steps of: retrieving patient medical information about the patient, determining a heaith risk profile for the patient based on the retrieved patient medical information; tracking actions of a patient following an office visit and providing the patient with action-based rewards for future healthcare. 19. The method of claim 18, further including assigning a predetermined code associated with the preselected medical information to the patient medical information and determining the health risk profile based at least in part on the predetermined code. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the patient medical information is an uncoded natural language expression in a first language, the method further including: sending the patient medical information to a translation resource; receiving translated patient medical information from the translation resource, wherein the translated patient medical information is in a second language; sending the translated patient medical information to a coding resource; and receiving, from the coding resource, the predetermined code associated with the patient medical information.
